inlineContent.text: DotFaceLite provides components for face capture and related functionalities which are easy to integrate into an iOS application.

abstract.text: The data sample captured at specific time which serves as an input for the face auto capture process.
Non-UI component for the face auto capture process.
Geometric properties of the preview. All rectangles are defined relative to the preview container.
Configuration for components utilizing a camera.
Class responsible for the localization of user interface strings.
Facing of the camera on the device.
Configure thresholds for the quality attributes of the face auto capture result.
If the threshold property is set to nil, the corresponding quality attribute will not be validated.
Indicates how the layer displays video frames.
Representation of closed interval with value type
This class defines the different states that the UI can be in during the process.
Configuration options for the UI MagnifEye Liveness component.
This state is active right after the component is created for a short period of time.
Configuration options for the UI Face Auto Capture component.
Customization options for the MagnifEye liveness user interface.
Stateless class which can detect faces in an image.
Presets for the quality attributes thresholds of the face auto capture result.
Phase of the face auto capture process.
Class responsible for the initialization of IDV libraries -
Static class responsible for generating logs. You can configure log level.
The final result of the Multi-Range Liveness process.
Configuration options for the UI Multi-Range Liveness component.

method is called and before the process finishes.

Configuration options for the face auto capture process.
Implement this delegate to handle the Multi-Range Liveness events.

Customization options for the Multi-Range Liveness user interface.
The result returned from the processing of one image during the face auto capture process.
